title: Everyone Thought the Sun Was Fixed — The Qur'an Said It's Racing Through Space
_note: CINEMATIC, hook-first, 60s+. Footage = sun/galaxy/stars (no humans; verified). NO recital; voice speaks English. SOURCE: Surah Ya-Sin 36:38. SCIENCE: the Sun orbits the Milky Way at ~230 km/s (~828,000 km/h); one galactic orbit ~225-250 million years; the Sun will eventually exhaust its fuel ('resting place'). Honest framing. Safe-zone, legible captions.

segments.text: For thousands of years, humans believed the sun was fixed in the sky. The Quran said something different. And it turned out to be true.
It said the sun is not standing still. It is running, toward a resting place of its own.
Remember, back then, the greatest scholars on Earth taught that the sun circled a motionless Earth.
The idea that the sun itself was flying somewhere sounded absurd.
Then astronomers discovered the truth. The sun is racing through our galaxy at over eight hundred thousand kilometers per hour.
And it drags the Earth, and you, along with it, on a single lap around the galaxy that takes over two hundred million years.
The Quran even said the sun runs to a resting place. A final destination. Scientists agree the sun will one day burn out, and stop.
A book from the seventh century knew the sun was moving. Science took thirteen hundred years to catch up.
And the sun runs toward its resting place. That is the design of the Almighty, the All-Knowing.
It was never standing still. And neither are you.

sources: Qur'an, Surah Ya-Sin 36:38 — 'And the sun runs to a resting place for it; that is the determination of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.'

Astronomy: the Sun orbits the galactic center at ~230 km/s (~828,000 km/h); one galactic orbit ≈ 225-250 million years; the Sun will exhaust its fuel in ~5 billion years (a 'resting place'). The Sun is not stationary — historically thought fixed.
Qur'an 36:38 shown Arabic + English; not recited. All Pexels clips verified human-free.
